title: CI verify job must fetch full history — the OKF pin suite asserts git-resolution a shallow clone cannot reach

trace.outcome: The release CI went red on the OKF pin suite's first-ever CI run: the suite verifies pins resolve through git, and the default shallow depth-1 checkout cannot reach the old pinned blobs, so 14 assertions that pass on full history fail on CI. Reproduced deterministically — a depth-1 clone fails 14/469, a full clone passes 471. Fixed with fetch-depth: 0 on the verify checkout; the fix gives the suite the history it asserts against without weakening it, and the committed-copy fallback that keeps a real shallow host working is untouched and separately tested.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "The pushed release v1.11.0 turned the Linux CI (verify) job RED on scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs — 14 of 469 assertions — while local verify was green across all 83 suites, and Windows CI (which runs only the portable subset, not the pin suite) passed. Root cause established by measurement, not inference: scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs did NOT exist at v1.10.0 nor at the old origin HEAD (git cat-file -e both report ABSENT), so the entire OKF pin system rode in the 59 commits that were never pushed until this release — its first-ever CI run. The suite verifies each content-addressed pin resolves through git (`git show <blob>:<path>`, assertion 'the unrepaired pin still resolves via git' at test_okf_pins.mjs:502, and 'the provenance blob is readable from git' at :1499), and the default actions/checkout@v4 is a shallow depth-1 clone that cannot reach the old pinned blobs. Reproduced locally and deterministically: a `git clone --depth 1` of this repo runs `node scripts/test_okf_pins.mjs` -> 'FAIL: 14 of 469 assertions failed' (the exact CI number), while a full `git clone` of the same repo -> 'PASS: 471 assertions'. Depth is the only variable.",
  "this_is_the_class": "local green is worthless if the suite never ran on the real environment: the OKF pin suite was green locally for the whole build because local has full history, and only the first push revealed it had never worked on a shallow CI clone. Production is unaffected — the pin resolver has a committed-copy fallback (source_copy + git hash-object) that keeps a genuine --depth 1 host working, and that fallback path has its own passing test (section 8, 'shallow-clone fallback ... PASSES'); what the shallow CI clone cannot do is exercise the git-resolution assertions the suite ALSO makes on purpose.",
  "fix": "ci.yml's checkout gains `fetch-depth: 0`, giving the verify job the full history the pin suite asserts against — making CI match the full-clone local run. This does NOT weaken the test: the git-resolution assertions are a deliberate part of what the suite verifies, and you cannot verify git-resolution without git history. The committed-copy fallback that protects a real shallow host is untouched and still tested. windows.yml runs only the portable subset (no pin suite) so it needs no change.",
  "green_evidence": "Full clone (the environment fetch-depth: 0 produces) -> PASS test_okf_pins: 471 assertions. ci.yml parses valid (python3 yaml.safe_load). The shipped code is unchanged by this fix — it is a CI-environment correction, so v1.11.0's deliverable is identical; only the config that validates it changed.",
  "orchestrator_note": "The verify for a CI-YAML change cannot be run as a local suite (CI is the environment under change); the evidence is the deterministic shallow-vs-full reproduction plus YAML validity, and the full local chain remaining green. The tag v1.11.0 (~10 minutes old, unpropagated — host propagation was removed and hosts update manually) is moved to include this fix rather than minting a v1.11.1 whose only content would be 'CI fetches full history', which would misrepresent a code change in the version history."
}

PASS test_okf_pins: 471 assertions — pins are content-addressed and fully asserted (sha, scheme, counts), empty/mismatched/unresolvable/unscheme'd extractions all exit 1, the committed-source fallback verifies via blob hash, onboarding.md's unparsed-block count is non-zero (format-blindness is visible), and the three schemes stay strict no-ops on each other's pins (narrative-sections derives a source's own "## " headings, refuses a heading-less source and a slug collision, and never touches the nine numbered pins)
